tweets.text:
Here's my analysis of the Strait of Hormuz situation for the last 24 hours.

As I noted yesterday, I was seeing a lot of vessels using the Oman route to exit. This was following the announcement by Oman and IMO. The IRGC retailiated today on a vessel, but even after the attack, I am still seeing transits. It's a lot less, but there are vessels willing to take the risk.

On the inbound front, the flow is still heavily restricted. Whatever outflow we are seeing today is unsustainable as there are not enough non-Iranian tankers going in. In particular, we need empty VLCCs going in to load up crude. This is just a trickle so far.

In my view, the traffic in the Oman lane will lead to more escalation by IRGC. Without throttling flows entirely in the Southern lane, IRGC will lose control of its leverage over the Strait. If they act, then it's a question of what the US does after.

I think this is only the beginning.

I have included the time lapse video with names using @MarineTraffic data and the list of inbound tankers.

On the inbound front, the flow is still heavily restricted. Whatever outflow we are seeing today is unsustainable as there are not enough non-Iranian tankers going in. In particular, we need empty VLCCs going in to load up crude. This is just a trickle so far.

In my view, the traffic in the Oman lane will lead to more escalation by IRGC. Without throttling flows entirely in the Southern lane, IRGC will lose control of its leverage over the Strait. If they act, then it's a question of what the US does after.

We got that sweep to 58k on high volume and long liqs. Lots of shorts came in on that last leg lower as well.

If $BTC could close above 60k today that would surely mean that it printed a local bottom today. https://t.co/AEYjfky8SM

Demand came in at 60k and there's a significant amount of shorts that are in an awkward spot rn.

However, we also have a perfect equal low now that's a prime target for hunting. Trading no man's land IMO. It would be ideal if we got a serious sweep to 58k.

$BTC https://t.co/qWnvEP8sHT

so @goodalexander is my trusted ai quant. his project sits at the top of task node, and it's the best way to get real exposure to what he's working on, plus his epic rants that never go public.

tweets.full_text: @PostFiatOrg

tweets.text:
so @goodalexander is my trusted ai quant. his project sits at the top of task node, and it's the best way to get real exposure to what he's working on, plus his epic rants that never go public.

for anyone who doesn't know the project: @postfiat is a layer 1 built for capital markets and collective intelligence. 

task node is the contributor network on top of it. hold a verified badge and the system routes you real work, pays you in $pft when it's done and verified. not a faucet, not farming. actual tasks that need doing.

work falls into a few buckets: amplification, code review, qa, and expert bundles. most of what i've done is qa, going through the product, documenting friction and broken flows, submitting evidence, and earning tokens when it's reviewed.

been on it a couple weeks and it's good. what matters to me: the work is real and payment clears onchain. you're not betting on a story, you're doing work and getting paid for it.

if you want in: https://t.co/qbyEJJevxs. set up a wallet, run personal tasks to build history, and network tasks open up from there.
